# `liber:resources@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:resources` package (`resources.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem resources package - resource accounting and budgeting.

## Imports

- `error` from [`liber:base@1`](/docs/gen/liber/base/v1.md)

## Types

### enum `resource-type`

The types of resource the kernel accounts and the ResourceManager budgets per Domain: physical memory held, live handles, live threads, in-transit IPC message bytes, pinned DMA memory, and user stack (whose limit is the per-thread ceiling). The typed vocabulary a budget is written in - each names a counter the kernel already charges and enforces; the policy only sets the cap.

| case | ordinal | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `memory` | 0 |  |
| `handles` | 1 |  |
| `threads` | 2 |  |
| `ipc-queue` | 3 |  |
| `dma` | 4 |  |
| `stack` | 5 |  |

### record `resource-usage`

One line of a Domain's budget: a resource type, how much of it is currently used, and the cap (u64::MAX means uncapped). The live used/limit pair the kernel tracks for one of a Domain's counters, read back over the typed API so usage is observable.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `type` | `resource-type` |  |
| `used` | `u64` |  |
| `limit` | `u64` |  |

### record `budget`

A Domain's budget: its name and the live usage of every accounted resource. The typed form of one Domain's resource account - what the `usage` command renders and what `set-limit` adjusts. A budget groups the per-Domain limits a policy assigns (e.g. all apps share one memory budget).

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | `string` |  |
| `usage` | `list<resource-usage>` |  |

## Interfaces

### interface `resources`

The ResourceManager contract: the policy over the kernel's resource accounting. `usage` returns the live budget of every managed Domain (used vs limit per resource, read straight from the kernel's counters); `set-limit` adjusts one Domain's cap for one resource at runtime and returns its updated budget. The kernel enforces the caps - an over-budget allocation fails with a typed error, contained to the offending Domain - this interface is the policy that sets and observes them.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `usage` | `usage() -> result<list<budget>, error>` |  |
| 2 | `set-limit` | `set-limit(name: string, type: resource-type, limit: u64) -> result<budget, error>` |  |
